page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8ae.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the x86_64 architecture, does not include local storage, and has no GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the processor demonstrates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing it in the top 10% tier. However, multi-core scaling is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling into the average range. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector extensions (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and hardware-accelerated cryptography (AES, VAES, and SHA_NI), which assist in processing specialized workloads despite the lack of AMX support.

Performance tradeoffs are evident in multi-threaded decompression and database operations. While single-threaded gzip and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark database operations register in the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are relatively weak. These characteristics suggest that while the instance offers high memory density and strong single-thread compute capabilities, it is less suited for highly parallelized database or high-throughput web serving tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r8ae.xlarge provides a dense memory profile relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing or local storage. It is best suited for applications such as in-memory caching, moderate data processing, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8ae.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on a 3.4 GHz AMD EPYC 9T34 processor and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance, though multi-core scaling and database operations fall into average and weaker percentiles respectively. The CPU supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration flags, enhancing its capability for specialized instruction sets. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and strong single-thread execution, the ecs.r8ae.xlarge is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, in-memory caching, and moderate data processing tasks that do not demand high parallel write throughput or local disk storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8ae.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8ae.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, high memory density, and strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz AMD EPYC 9T34 processor
32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration flags
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for in-memory caching and memory-intensive applications
